# KBC Financial Operations Update

Purpose: A plain-language one-page handout for the church.

Status: Draft for congregational sharing

## Why This Matters

KBC wants to handle the church's finances with faithfulness, care, and clear accountability. The current Treasurer transition gives us an opportunity to strengthen the system so the work is easier to understand, easier to review, and easier to hand off in the future.

This is not about blaming any person or committee. It is about making sure the church has a healthy and sustainable way to manage financial responsibilities.

## What We Are Trying To Fix

The Treasurer role has grown broad over time. It can include both officer-level responsibility and many day-to-day bookkeeping tasks.

That makes the role harder to fill and harder for one person to carry. It also makes transitions more difficult when a Treasurer needs to step down or when a new person begins serving.

The goal is to make responsibilities clearer, better documented, and more sustainable.

## Proposed Direction

Leadership is reviewing a structure that would keep the Treasurer as an accountable elected officer while also considering separate bookkeeping or financial administration support.

The Treasurer would remain responsible for financial accountability, review, reporting, and coordination with the Finance Committee.

A Bookkeeper or Financial Administrator, if approved, could help with routine financial work such as records, deposits, reimbursements, reports, and documentation.

The Finance Committee and Personnel Committee would each review the parts of the plan that belong to them before any final recommendation comes to the church.

## How Work Will Be Divided

- The Treasurer would focus on accountability, review, and reporting.
- The Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator would focus on routine financial administration if the role is approved.
- The Finance Committee would review financial controls, software, reimbursements, budget matters, and monthly reports.
- The Personnel Committee would review job description, application, hiring, compensation, and evaluation questions.
- The church would vote on matters that require congregational approval.

## What Comes Next

Leadership will review the proposed structure and identify which questions need committee review.

The Finance Committee will review financial process questions, including software, reimbursements, spending authority, monthly reporting, and financial review.

The Personnel Committee will review any proposed bookkeeping role, including job description, application process, supervision, and compensation questions.

After those reviews, any recommendation requiring church approval will be brought to the congregation in the proper way.

## What The Church Is Being Asked To Support

At this stage, the church is being asked to support a careful review process.

This means supporting:

- A clearer and more sustainable financial operations structure.
- A healthy distinction between Treasurer responsibilities and routine bookkeeping work.
- Committee review before final recommendations are made.
- Good stewardship, good documentation, and good accountability.
- Future church votes where bylaws, budget, policy, or church practice require them.

No final staffing, budget, software, or policy decision should be considered approved until the proper review and approval steps are complete.
